name: tdd-vitest-typescript
description: Test-Driven Development (TDD) using Vitest and TypeScript. Use when the user requests help with TDD, writing tests before code, test-first development, Vitest test setup, TypeScript testing patterns, unit testing, integration testing, or following the Red-Green-Refactor cycle with Vitest.
capabilities: Guide Red-Green-Refactor TDD workflow with Vitest
Set up Vitest configuration for TypeScript projects
Write unit tests, integration tests, and async tests
Implement mocking and spying with Vitest test utilities
Identify and avoid common testing anti-patterns
Apply advanced testing patterns for complex scenarios
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: Legitimate TDD educational skill. Static analyzer flagged benign code patterns: TypeScript template literals misidentified as shell backticks, regex validation patterns misidentified as weak crypto, and project scaffolding fs operations. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Writing tests before code ensures reliable, maintainable software. This skill guides you through the Red-Green-Refactor cycle with Vitest, covering test patterns, mocking strategies, and TDD best practices.
userTitle: Set up TDD with Vitest and TypeScript
limitations: Does not execute tests directly in user environment
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines
Does not provide pre-configured testing frameworks beyond Vitest
Focus is on testing methodology, not specific application domains

useCases.title: Starting a New Feature with TDD
useCases.description: A developer wants to build a new feature using Test-Driven Development. The skill guides them through writing failing tests first, implementing minimal code, and refactoring while keeping tests green.

useCases.title: Learning TypeScript Testing Patterns
useCases.description: A developer new to TypeScript testing wants to learn patterns for type-safe test data, generic function testing, and proper mocking strategies with Vitest.

useCases.title: Improving Test Quality
useCases.description: A team wants to improve their test suite. The skill helps identify anti-patterns like testing implementation details, complex setup, and vague test names.

promptTemplates.title: Basic TDD Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up Test-Driven Development using Vitest and TypeScript. I want to follow the Red-Green-Refactor cycle for building a new feature.

promptTemplates.title: Writing Failing Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a failing test first for a Calculator class with add, subtract, multiply, and divide methods. Then show me the minimal implementation to make the tests pass.

promptTemplates.title: Mocking Dependencies
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I mock an external API dependency in Vitest? I need to test a service that calls a remote API without making actual network requests.

promptTemplates.title: Avoiding Testing Anti-Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my test code and identify any anti-patterns. My tests are brittle and break when I refactor even though the behavior is unchanged.

bestPractices: Write failing tests first before any implementation code to define behavior clearly
Keep tests focused with single assertions per test concern for easier debugging
Test public behavior and outcomes rather than private implementation details
antiPatterns: Testing implementation details like private methods that may change during refactoring
Using arbitrary sleep or timeout delays in tests instead of waiting for actual conditions
Creating massive test setup with dozens of lines of boilerplate instead of reusable helpers

faq.question: What is the TDD cycle?
faq.answer: TDD follows three phases: Red (write a failing test), Green (write minimal code to pass), and Refactor (improve code while keeping tests green). This cycle ensures tests define behavior before implementation.

faq.question: How do I run Vitest in watch mode?
faq.answer: Run 'vitest' without arguments to start watch mode. Tests automatically re-run when files change, enabling rapid TDD iterations.

faq.question: What test file naming convention does Vitest use?
faq.answer: Vitest recognizes files named *.test.ts or *.spec.ts. Place tests adjacent to source files or in __tests__ directories.

faq.question: How do I mock a module in Vitest?
faq.answer: Use vi.mock() before imports: 'vi.mock("./api");'. Then use vi.mocked() to type mock returns or vi.fn() for spy functions.

faq.question: What coverage target should I aim for?
faq.answer: Aim for 80%+ coverage on business logic and 100% on critical paths like authentication. Avoid obsessing over 100% coverage everywhere.

faq.question: How do I test async code with Vitest?
faq.answer: Use async/await with expect(result).rejects.toThrow() for rejected promises, or use .resolves for fulfilled promises.
